summary: Antonelli controlled the Belgian Grand Prix from pole position to the finish line, extending his championship lead while his Mercedes teammate Russell retired from fourth after a crash. The race pivoted when Russell's early exit left Antonelli unchallenged at the front, while Norris fought through a 10-place grid penalty to salvage points in the midfield. Antonelli's victory stretches his lead in the standings as Mercedes consolidates its position atop the constructors' championship.
